headline: How private credit's cracks are threatening to deepen private equity's woes
summary: Private credit's rapid rise was key to global dealmaking over the past decade. Now, signs of strain in the $3 trillion market are spilling into private equity.

headline: JPMorgan's CEO has an urgent and concerning message for investors
summary: JPMorgan Chase just reported strong first-quarter results. Its CEO used the earnings call to warn investors not to get comfortable. On JPMorgan's Q1 2026 earnings call on April 14, CEO Jamie Dimon declined to predict whether the U.S. was heading for a recession. But he did not hold back on what ...

headline: Short Supply Hits Silver for 6th Straight Year: 2 Winning Stocks to Own Now
summary: Silver enters 2026 facing its sixth straight annual structural deficit. The Silver Institute’s World Silver Survey 2026 projects a 46.3 million-ounce shortfall—15% wider than the 40.3 million ounces recorded in 2025. Total supply is seen slipping 2% while demand eases only modestly. Above-ground stocks have already shed a cumulative 762 million ounces since 2021 to ... Short Supply Hits Silver for 6th Straight Year: 2 Winning Stocks to Own Now

headline: Why The Vår Energi (OB:VAR) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Targets And Assumptions
summary: Vår Energi sits at the center of an active debate, with price targets ranging from NOK 30 at the bearish end to NOK 44 and NOK 41 in more recent revisions. Those swings, along with successive moves such as JPMorgan lifting its target first to NOK 40 and then to NOK 44, reflect how analysts are constantly reassessing valuation against perceived execution and risk. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets can help you track the evolving narrative around the shares. Analyst Price...

headline: Option Traders Chasing Torrid Stock Rally Turn Focus to Earnings
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Option traders have flipped from concern that the Iran war will sink the economy to fear of missing out on the torrid equity rebound. headline: How The Investment Story For Shell (LSE:SHEL) Is Shifting As Analyst Views Split
summary: Shell’s updated fair value estimate has shifted from £34.65 to £37.17, giving you a fresh reference point for how analysts are framing the shares today. Behind that move, recent research has featured both higher and lower price targets, with some firms tying upgrades to revised commodity assumptions, while others trim targets or ratings on concerns that a lot of good news may already be reflected. Read on to see how these diverging calls fit together and how you can keep track as the...
